summary.intent: Merge origin/main into the skill-creation branch, resolve all conflicts, and push the result to the remote.
summary.outcome: Successfully merged main into the branch and pushed after resolving 7 conflict files, refactoring trait locations, and fixing biome/rustfmt formatting issues caught by local CI.

repo: CHANGELOG.md is auto-generated by git-cliff; HEAD entries should be discarded in favor of main's version during merges.

The SkillsService trait and other service traits were moved from gateway/src/services.rs to a dedicated crates/service-traits/src/lib.rs crate ('moltis-service-traits') as part of a main-branch refactor.
Old gateway-level websocket.js and methods.rs were deleted in main and replaced by crates/web/src/assets/js/websocket.js and crates/gateway/src/methods/ respectively.
RPC methods must be registered in two places: the handler in crates/gateway/src/methods/services.rs AND the allowed-methods list in crates/gateway/src/methods/mod.rs.
Biome is used for JS/TS formatting; rustfmt (nightly-2025-11-30) is used for Rust; both are enforced by local-validate.sh.
The project uses a scripts/local-validate.sh script that validates against a specific PR number and publishes commit statuses to GitHub.

code.finding: SkillsService trait lives here (not in gateway/src/services.rs). New trait methods like skill_save must be added here along with a NoopSkillsStub implementation.
File begins with re-exports from moltis-service-traits crate. Gateway-specific NoopSkillsService impl follows. A spurious blank line before the comment triggered rustfmt failure.
Contains an explicit allowlist of mutating RPC method names. skills.skill.save must be added here to be routable.
skills.skill.save RPC handler was added here (replaces the old methods.rs pattern).
Skill hint display logic belongs in completeToolCard (not inline in handleChatToolCallEnd). navigate import must also be added here.
Biome enforces multi-line formatting for chained method calls and object argument literals; inline one-liners for sendRpc and toolsRaw.split().map().filter() are rejected.

workflow: When a remote branch has diverged from local (e.g. remote has commits not in local merge), a second merge of the remote branch into local is needed before pushing — rebase in this scenario replays all commits and causes massive conflicts.
After resolving modify/delete conflicts (files moved in main), you must explicitly git rm the old paths to mark them resolved rather than just editing.
Python one-liners via Bash are a fallback for resolving conflicts in large files where Edit tool context-matching fails.
local-validate.sh catches formatting issues that cargo build does not; always run it before considering the branch clean.

friction: The Edit tool failed on services.rs conflicts due to file size, requiring a Python-based sed workaround.
Duplicate trait definitions in services.rs (HEAD had them inline, main moved them to moltis-service-traits) caused a structural mismatch that required significant detective work to diagnose.
The remote branch had 2 commits not present in the local merge, causing push rejection and requiring a second merge commit.
Attempting git pull --rebase after the merge commit triggered a catastrophic rebase that had to be aborted.
No origin remote was configured, making it impossible to inspect main's file content directly via git show — required using FETCH_HEAD and merge-base heuristics.
ServiceError type changed in main, breaking the skill_save impl and requiring a separate fix pass.
Tailwind CSS rebuild was required after adding new CSS classes from conflict resolution.
open_items: Verify that NoopSkillsService implements all new methods added to SkillsService in main (security_status, security_scan, skill_trust, emergency_disable) — confirmed present but not tested.
The websocket.js navigate import and skill hint code added to completeToolCard were not covered by any automated test in this session.
stash containing .superset/config.json was consumed by the merge process — unclear if the file's changes were preserved correctly.
